title: Un rinforzo per Alessio Lisci. Il PAOK Salonicco annuncia l'acquisto di Elustondo
excerpt: Rinforzo per Alessio Lisci, nuovo allenatore del PAOK.
summary: Rinforzo per Alessio Lisci, nuovo allenatore del PAOK. Il club di Saloinicco ha annunciato l'acquisto di Aritz Elustondo. 32 anni, difensore centrale, all'occorrenza terzino destro, il giocatore arriva a titolo gratuito dopo la fine del suo contratto con la Real Sociedad. Accordo fino al 2028. Visualizza questo post su Instagram Un post condiviso da PAOK FC (@paok_fc)

title: Fiorentina, passi avanti di Beltran verso il River Plate: cosa manca per chiudere la trattativa
excerpt: La Fiorentina è pronta ad ufficializzare il suo primo acquisto della sessione estiva.
summary: La Fiorentina è pronta ad ufficializzare il suo primo acquisto della sessione estiva. Si tratta del difensore brasiliano Viery, prossimo a vestire la maglia viola. Come vi abbiamo raccontato (QUI la news), il giocatore nella giornata di martedì 30 giugno atterrerà a Roma e, dopo aver svolto le visite mediche, firmerà il contratto che lo legherà al club toscano per i prossimi cinque anni. Ma la Fiorentina è particolarmente attiva anche sul fronte cessioni e con il passare del tempo prende sempre più quota il ritorno al River Plate dell'attaccante Lucas Beltran. Seppur il Vikingo in un primo momento abbia detto 'no' all'ipotesi di tornare in Sudamerica, nel club che lo ha lanciato, nelle ultime ore la posizione del giocatore si è ammorbidita e adesso ci sono tutti i crismi per cui la trattativa possa andare a dama. Secondo quanto raccolto dalla redazione di TuttoMercatoWeb, il club viola e i Millonarios stanno proseguendo con i dialoghi e Beltran ha fatto ulteriori passi avanti verso un ritorno al Monumental. Manca ancora qualcosa per la definitiva fumata bianca, ma c'è fiducia che alla fine la Fiorentina possa chiudere l'accordo sulla base di un'offerta da 7.5 milioni più il 50% della futura rivendita.

title: Vis Pesaro, Bosco: "Prossimo anno budget ridotto. Se si vuol contestare è meglio stare a casa"
excerpt: “La 2025-2026 è stata la miglior stagione della Vis di sempre.
summary: “La 2025-2026 è stata la miglior stagione della Vis di sempre. Lo è stato sia per il V-Park che è un centro sportivo d’eccellenza in Italia e lo è stato per la valorizzazione dei giovani come Mariani. La vendita sua e di altri a club di Serie A o Serie B dovrà essere motivo d’orgoglio per una società come la nostra”. Il presidente della Vis Pesaro Mauro Bosco ha parlato così in conferenza stampa nella giornata di ieri facendo il punto sull’ultima annata per poi concentrarsi sul futuro. “Dalla prossima stagione entrerà ufficialmente in vigore il salary cap, meccanismo che io ho appoggiato per un calcio più sostenibile e per il quale negli emolumenti, non si può superare il 50% del costo di produzione. - prosegue Bosco come si legge sul Corriere Adriatico - Non possiamo dunque investire nella squadra più della metà dei ricavi e questi non sono dati trascurabili per noi visto che abbiamo una media di spettatori di 1700 persone, e merchandising e sponsorizzazioni sono trascurabili. Ringraziamo chi ci è vicino ma non preghiamo nessuno. Anche perché è una goccia nell’oceano”. Bosco poi parla della squadra che sarà: “Con un budget ridotto rispetto agli anni precedenti l’obiettivo sarà la salvezza ed per questo faccio un appello ai tifosi e gli dico che ci sarà da soffrire e bisognerà stare uniti e combattere tutti insieme. Se lo spirito non è quello, ma c’è voglia di contestazioni fuori luogo come quelle capitate nel finale di questa stagione, restate a casa. C’è Sky e c’è il divano...

title: Besiktas, Italiano valuterà Kilicsoy. E trova buone referenze da Pisacane
excerpt: Vincenzo Italiano guiderà dalla prossima stagione il Besiktas e tra i giocatori che dovrebbe avere in rosa c'è Semih Kilicsoy, reduce dal prestito al Cagliari e rientrato alla casa madre.
summary: Vincenzo Italiano guiderà dalla prossima stagione il Besiktas e tra i giocatori che dovrebbe avere in rosa c'è Semih Kilicsoy, reduce dal prestito al Cagliari e rientrato alla casa madre. In merito il nuovo tecnico ha chiesto informazioni sul giocatore, trovando buoni riscontri in Fabio Pisacane, che lo ha guidato nel suo primo nonché unico anno in Sardegna. Pertanto Italiano terrà Kilicsoy, almeno in ritiro, per poterlo valutare. 20 anni, Kilicsoy ha raccolto 22 presenze con i rossoblù e segnato 4 gol. Il Cagliari aveva un'opzione di acquisto di 12 milioni di euro, che ha deciso di non esercitare.

title: Il Mondiale esalta Infantino: "Fase a gironi incredibile! È lo spettacolo più grande del pianeta"
excerpt: La fase a gironi del Mondiale è ormai alle spalle e l’entusiasmo attorno alla competizione continua a crescere con l’avvicinarsi dei sedicesimi di finale.
summary: La fase a gironi del Mondiale è ormai alle spalle e l’entusiasmo attorno alla competizione continua a crescere con l’avvicinarsi dei sedicesimi di finale. Un’edizione che, fin dalle prime giornate, ha fatto registrare numeri e partecipazione senza precedenti, confermando l’impatto globale dell’evento. Infantino ha poi rimarcato anche il livello tecnico e spettacolare della competizione, soffermandosi sui protagonisti e sui record già stabiliti: "Sono stati battuti numerosi record grazie a campioni del calibro di Lionel Messi e Cristiano Ronaldo, tra gli altri, mentre nuovi eroi sono emersi da tantissime nazioni diverse. Un numero record di gol ha regalato grandi emozioni ai nostri incredibili tifosi, mentre continuiamo a mostrare il potere unificante del calcio. Questa è la Coppa del Mondo FIFA 2026. Ed è lo spettacolo più grande del pianeta!".

title: Inter move to lock down Pio Esposito as Napoli & Man Utd background revealed
excerpt: Inter are moving to secure the long-term future of Pio Esposito, with transfer journalist Alfredo...
summary: Inter are moving to secure the long-term future of Pio Esposito, with transfer journalist Alfredo Pedulla reporting the Nerazzurri are ready to commit firmly to the striker who turns 21 today. It caps a breakout campaign for the forward, who returned from a prolific loan at Spezia to score seven Serie A goals and play his part in Inter’s domestic double. He also broke into the senior Italy setup, scoring against Greece in a recent friendly, a rare bright spot for an Azzurri side that missed this summer’s World Cup.

title: Mertesacker on Italy’s World Cup absence: ”They don’t deserve to be talked about’
excerpt: Per Mertesacker has delivered a blunt verdict on Italy’s modern decline, telling German broadcaster ZDF...
summary: Per Mertesacker has delivered a blunt verdict on Italy’s modern decline, telling German broadcaster ZDF that the four-time world champions no longer warrant discussion at a tournament they have again failed to reach. The former Arsenal and Germany defender appeared alongside ex-Germany international Christoph Kramer on Friday evening, where the presenter raised the question of the Azzurri’s absence from the 2026 World Cup; the third successive edition Italy have missed. “We’ve never talked, during these weeks, about what a shame it is that Italy aren’t taking part,” she put to the pair, with quotes via CalcioMercato.com.

title: Modric denies talks of Mourinho reunion at Real Madrid: ‘I wish him the best’
excerpt: Luka Modric is into the final few days of his contract with Milan, and despite...
summary: Luka Modric is into the final few days of his contract with Milan, and despite reports linking him with a return to his beloved Real Madrid this summer, the Croatian has denied having any contact with Jose Mourinho over a reunion at the Bernabeu. Modric continues to defy his age, becoming the oldest player ever to provide an assist at the World Cup, setting up the winner for Torino’s Nikola Vlasic at 40 years old during Croatia’s 2-1 win over Ghana in Group L on Saturday night. The result means that Modric and Croatia progress to the round of 32 as the second-placed team in Group L. They will go on to face a Portugal squad featuring Rafael Leao and Francisco Conceicao on Friday evening.

title: Which Serie A side has the most representatives left at the 2026 World Cup?
excerpt: The 2026 World Cup group stage is finally complete, the line-up for the first ever...
summary: The 2026 World Cup group stage is finally complete, the line-up for the first ever round of 32 has been set, 19 Serie A and Serie B clubs are still represented at the tournament, but no other Italian side boasts more stars than Milan, who have 10. The World Cup group stage came to a close in the early hours of Sunday morning, ending with a 3-3 draw between Algeria and Austria and a 3-1 victory for an Argentina side featuring Lautaro Martinez and Nico Paz against Jordan. 48 teams have been cut down to 32 and those sides will now head into the knock-out bracket, which kicks off with South Africa vs. co-hosts Canada at 20.00 on Sunday evening.

title: 64 Serie A players left standing at the 2026 World Cup – Who are you supporting?
excerpt: 64 Italian-based players are left standing at the 2026 World Cup heading into the knock-out...
summary: 64 Italian-based players are left standing at the 2026 World Cup heading into the knock-out rounds, and with no Italy at the tournament, Croatia might be the team for the Serie A neutral to support in the round of 32. But we want to know who you will be supporting for the remainder of the summer – Let us know below! Croatia feature more Serie A or Serie B representatives than any other nation left at the World Cup with a total of seven, namely Milan’s Luka Modric, Inter’s Petar Sucic, Como’s Martin Baturina, Atalanta’s Mario Pasalic, Torino’s Nikola Vlasic, Bologna’s Nikola Moro and Fiorentina’s Marin Pongracic. Belgium and Norway hold the second-most Serie A representatives in the remaining World Cup squads with five apiece, the Belgian selections being headlined by Napoli pair Kevin De Bruyne and Romelu Lukaku. Norway’s Leo Ostigard was the first Serie A player to score at the 2026 World Cup.

title: Clarke leaves strong Scotland legacy but exit brings sense of relief
excerpt: Steve Clarke took Scotland out of the wilderness - but not past the group stage of a tournament. Tom English's must-read analysis of his reign.
summary: For much of Saturday afternoon, the skies above Charlotte were a dirty shade of grey, the rain bucketing down, the wind blowing hard, thunder and lightning seen and heard every minute or so. If Steve Clarke was looking out of his hotel room, that's the biblical scene he would have taken in. In a sense, the ideal backdrop for a bombshell. Within minutes of Scotland being officially dumped out of the World Cup, the news of the head coach's departure dropped. Unlike the weather, there was no warning.

title: How Bellingham became Tuchel's most important player
excerpt: His starting place was the subject of much debate in Thomas Tuchel's England team, but Jude Bellingham has become integral to the manager's style of play.
summary: Jude Bellingham's tactical adaptability has become key to Thomas Tuchel's style of play with this England team In June 2024, Jude Bellingham scored a dramatic overhead kick in the 95th minute against Slovakia to rescue England's hopes at the Euros. In the heat of the moment, he celebrated by screaming into the jubilant crowd.

title: Are Iran the unluckiest side in World Cup history?
excerpt: Iran had a place in the last 32 of the World Cup snatched away from them at the last minute not once but twice.
summary: Iran missed out on a place in the last 32 of the World Cup on goal difference For every moment of glory, there is a hard-luck story, a nation left to bemoan their misfortune and the injustices that have led to their downfall. Yet few compare to the cruel manner that brought Iran's tournament to an end in 2026.

title: Derided by Leeds, snubbed by USA - but Marsch becoming Canada hero
excerpt: Derided by Leeds fans and snubbed by the USA, Jesse Marsch has faced difficult moments in his career - but appears to have found his home with Canada as they look to reach the World Cup last 16.
summary: Jesse Marsch's name will forever be in the history books. He has earned sporting immortality by leading Canada into the knockouts of a World Cup for the first time - and on Sunday they will look to go further when they face South Africa in Los Angeles for a place in the last 16. It is an impressive achievement for a coach who has overcome some difficult moments in his career.

excerpt: Spain winger Nico Williams said he had suffered "one of the worst days of my life" after an injury against Uruguay on Friday jeopardized his future participation in the World Cup.

title: World Cup 2026 power rankings: France still kings but who has climbed 26 places?
excerpt:
From Algeria to Uzbekistan, we assess the standing of the 48 nations after the group stage of the tournament

It took a little over an hour for Kylian Mbappé to find his groove. Irked by a poor refereeing decision, he
summary: From Algeria to Uzbekistan, we assess the standing of the 48 nations after the group stage of the tournament It took a little over an hour for Kylian Mbappé to find his groove. Irked by a poor refereeing decision, he scored twice in France’s opener against Senegal. Ably supported by a stunning cast, Mbappé will already have his sights on winning the competition. Michael Olise has shown his class throughout, while Ousmane Dembélé dazzled with a hat-trick against Norway. We’ll see if anyone can stop Didier Deschamps’ team. Lionel Messi turned 39 this week but there is little indication he is slowing down. A missed penalty against Austria was the only blemish on his Group J record and, to be fair, he scored twice in that match, adding to his hat-trick against Algeria as he became the World Cup’s record goalscorer. Argentina’s success will hinge on their captain, which may explain why he was allowed a breather in the final group match – although he still came off the bench to score his sixth goal in the group stage.

title: Iran needed help to save their ‘disaster’ World Cup. Instead they got more heartbreak
excerpt:
Team Melli’s agonizing exit after other results failed to break their way is a moment of anguish that rises to the top of a football history full of them

Mehdi Taremi lay on the ground at Seattle Stadium staring at the heavens, devastated. The Iran team he captains had just finished
summary: Team Melli’s agonizing exit after other results failed to break their way is a moment of anguish that rises to the top of a football history full of them Mehdi Taremi lay on the ground at Seattle Stadium staring at the heavens, devastated. The Iran team he captains had just finished a 1-1 draw with Egypt in their final World Cup group game – one where a win would have put them through to the knockout round for the first time in seven tournament appearances. Taremi had missed a penalty in Friday’s contest, and a seeming late winner had sent the team into bedlam before being correctly ruled out for offside. Taremi lay on the ground for several minutes. He did so as Alireza Jahanbakhsh, his longtime teammate on a veteran-laden Iran squad, offered comfort. Taremi remained after other teammates who had collapsed in exhaustion had arisen again, trudging to the locker room.

summary: ‘The bigger the games get, the bigger we will get,’ he says Jarell Quansah hopes to be fit for last 32 after ankle sprain Thomas Tuchel believes England can only get better after sealing top spot in Group L and has backed his side to “step up” for the knockout stages of the World Cup.

title: World Cup glory could all come down to penalties. What’s the best strategy?
excerpt:
As the knockout stages begin, penalties are inevitable. What do the stats say about the best ways to take them?

The 2022 World Cup final was a nail-biting affair, decided by a penalty sh
summary: As the knockout stages begin, penalties are inevitable. What do the stats say about the best ways to take them? The 2022 World Cup final was a nail-biting affair, decided by a penalty shootout after Argentina and France were evenly matched with three goals apiece. Argentina subsequently won 4-2 on penalties, taking the title. Even outside penalty shootouts, a single spot kick can easily decide the outcome of a game at a World Cup, making any advantage in taking a penalty crucial. So what can we learn about the best strategy for penalty kicks from statistics and research?

title: La Hypermotion sigue de moda: los dos finalistas de Champions, en la pretemporada de Segunda
excerpt: PSG y Arsenal se enfrentarán al Mallorca y al Girona, respectivamente, en el mes de agosto
summary: PSG y Arsenal se enfrentarán al Mallorca y al Girona, respectivamente, en el mes de agosto Acaba de terminar LaLiga Hypermotion 2025-2026, pero la 2026-2027 ya está dando que hablar. O más bien, su pretemporada. Porque los dos últimos finalistas de la Champions League jugarán este verano partidos amistosos contra dos equipos de la categoría de plata que acaban de descender de Primera división. El Girona recibirá al Arsenal, campeón de la Premier League, en la 49ª edición de un Trofeo Costa Brava que, además, conmemora el 150 aniversario de Estrella Damm. El encuentro, que servirá como presentación de los catalanes delante de su afición, se disputará el sábado 1 de agosto, a las 20 horas, en Montilivi. Hace año y medio, ambos se enfrentaron en la máxima competición continental, con triunfo londinense (1-2).

title: Barça y Ajax ya negocian: Ter Stegen les va a salir muy caro a los culés
excerpt: El club azulgrana pagará la mayor parte de la ficha aunque juegue cedido en Ámsterdam
summary: El club azulgrana pagará la mayor parte de la ficha aunque juegue cedido en Ámsterdam Barcelona y Ajax han comenzado a negociar la cesión de Ter Stegen al club neerlandés para la próxima temporada. La portería es una de las posiciones que el Ajax necesitaba reforzar de manera urgente y, con la llegada de Míchel al banquillo, la opción del portero alemán se ha reactivado. No hay que olvidar que el técnico ya lo pidió la temporada pasada para el Girona, aunque prácticamente no pudo contar con él debido a una nueva lesión. Ahora quiere llevárselo con él al Ajax. La negociación con el Barcelona no es sencilla porque Ter Stegen tiene una ficha muy elevada que el club neerlandés no puede asumir. No solo eso, sino que únicamente está dispuesto a pagar entre el 10 % y el 15 % del salario que percibe el guardameta por temporada. Una situación similar se produjo la pasada campaña, cuando se marchó cedido al Girona.

title: Ni un paso atrás con Julián
excerpt: Un mes después de que el jugador, su entorno y el Barça pusieron sobre la mesa la opción de que salga, el Atleti no se mueve de su postura: 500 millones o no saldrá
summary: Un mes después de que el jugador, su entorno y el Barça pusieron sobre la mesa la opción de que salga, el Atleti no se mueve de su postura: 500 millones o no saldrá Hace algo más de un mes MARCA desveló en exclusiva que Julián Álvarez le había comunicado al Atlético su voluntad de salir del club este verano. El run run sobre la figura de la Araña se convertía en realidad: la estrella rojiblanca quería salir y elegía un destino: el Barcelona. Su discurso, dar un salto en su carrera tras dos temporadas en el Metropolitano en las que no había podido sumar títulos a su extenso palmarés. Más de un mes después, decimos, la postura del Atlético sigue siendo la misma: si quiere salir su precio son 500 millones de euros. Ni las filtraciones de su entorno, ni el discurso del Barça dando por hecho que los rojiblancos le deben dejar salir por un precio sensiblemente inferior, ni siquiera las palabras del propio Julián pidiendo públicamente una “transferencia” han movido a los rojiblancos de su posición: no se mueve del club.

title: El gigante vikingo se devora un pastrami
summary: Durante su estancia en Nueva York, el noruego no desaprovechó la oportunidad de probar la gastronomía morbida de los Estados Unidos y se come un festín.

title: Enol Rodríguez se suma a un Córdoba sin Pedro Ortiz ni Xavi Sintes
excerpt: El club apuesta por el delantero procedente del Huesca para su ataque, mientras confirma dos nuevas bajas
summary: El club apuesta por el delantero procedente del Huesca para su ataque, mientras confirma dos nuevas bajas Enol Rodríguez, hasta ahora delantero del Huesca, es la sexta incorporación del Córdoba para la temporada 2025-26, tras las ya conocidas de Tasende (Zaragoza), Adnane Ghailan (Europa), Egoitz Muñoz (Alavés), Unai Sabroso (Rayo Vallecano B) y Jacobo Martí (Mérida). Está a punto de cumplir 25 años. El nuevo jugador cordobesista es asturiano, natural de Candás, y estuvo integrado en la cantera del Real Oviedo, con el que llegó a debutar en Segunda en la temporada 2022-23. Antes había pasado por Marino de Luanco y UD Logroñés B. Empezó a destacar en el Arenteiro en Primera Federación en la campaña 2024-25 y de ahí dio el salto al Huesca.

title: Bellingham and Kane strike as England seal top spot with World Cup win over Panama
excerpt: England are not going to win the World Cup playing like this. They will not get very far in the knockout rounds unless they can sharpen up. But at least they have made it into them, the dream still alive, after they secured top spot in Group L on a slow-burn occasion when the result meant everyth
summary: England are not going to win the World Cup playing like this. They will not get very far in the knockout rounds unless they can sharpen up. But at least they have made it into them, the dream still alive, after they secured top spot in Group L on a slow-burn occasion when the result meant everything. Thomas Tuchel needed to restore momentum after the bore draw against Ghana in game two, to rekindle the excitement from the opening game against Croatia, which England won 4-2. The manager wanted to prove a point. It did not really happen and for the opening 45 minutes it was even possible to fear the worst. Were England going to fail to prise apart another obdurate opponent? Tuchel’s team were better after the interval. They knew that they might need only one goal and relief was the overriding emotion when Jude Bellingham stepped up. It was an expertly steered low volley from Bukayo Saka’s corner for the breakthrough, although Tuchel’s reaction was instructive. He did not celebrate. He looked annoyed.

title: Late drama sends Austria and Algeria into World Cup knockouts to break Iran hearts
excerpt: Before a ball was kicked, this match already had nicknames. Some deemed it a telltale biscotto – an Italian classic implying something that has to be baked twice; one cooperating with the other. Or maybe you preferred the “Disgrace of Kansas City”, a new spin on the “Disgrace of Gijón”,
summary: Before a ball was kicked, this match already had nicknames. Some deemed it a telltale biscotto – an Italian classic implying something that has to be baked twice; one cooperating with the other. Or maybe you preferred the “Disgrace of Kansas City”, a new spin on the “Disgrace of Gijón”, the 1982 World Cup match in which West Germany settled for a 1-0 win over Austria in a result that qualified both teams for the knockout round while eliminating Algeria. But what unfolded here was something else altogether. A new classic of the genre. Call it the “Missouri Compromise” if you must, but even that somehow underplays the drama and wild swings of momentum that exhausted the players and coaches who took part, delighting the fans who were lucky enough to witness it. On a steamy night in the American midwest, Algeria and Austria battled gamely, showing heart and desire for most if not all 90-plus minutes en route to a back-and-forth 3-3 draw that qualified both teams for the knockout round. Their continued World Cup journeys came at the expense of Iran, who just needed a winner – any winner – to emerge in this wild match to keep their tournament alive. To have been a fly on the wall of their Tijuana hotel, watching as their World Cup fate teetered and tottered, their jubilation at Riyad Mahrez’s goal in the third minute of added time abruptly ended by a Sasa Kalajdzic header with practically the last action of the match.

title: Lionel Messi steps off bench and scores to cap Argentina’s World Cup win over Jordan
excerpt: Give Lionel Messi half an hour and he will sprinkle some magic to remember for ever. Most of the crowd under this vast roof had been afflicted with their own strain of Messidependencia for two-thirds of this game, whose low stakes had been reflected in both tempo and ambience. Eve
summary: Give Lionel Messi half an hour and he will sprinkle some magic to remember for ever. Most of the crowd under this vast roof had been afflicted with their own strain of Messidependencia for two-thirds of this game, whose low stakes had been reflected in both tempo and ambience. Everything before their idol’s arrival felt almost self-consciously like a prelude, played out in the certainty that a crescendo would follow. There was no need to worry about anything else here: why not get ready to bask in seeing a little more history being made? You can set your watch by Messi, even if that does not usually mean winding it to the 60th minute. He had agreed with Lionel Scaloni that, with Argentina already guaranteed the spoils in Group J, that he would not be unleashed until then. After all, no chances should be taken for the deadest of rubbers. From the moment he came on this felt like an occasion, a moment in lore, another precious chance to distil the admiration for a legend of advancing years. So Messi delivered and sent another flock of starry-eyed devotees beaming into the muggy Texas night. Messi had already wafted a free-kick too high, the distance unaccommodating, when he was pulled down 22 yards from goal. The position, almost dead centre, could not have been more inviting and its exploitation felt inevitable. Even so the outcome was slightly curious, Messi whipping the ball around Jordan’s wall with enough bend that it nestled in the middle of Yazeed Abulaila’s net. The kee...

title: Colombia take top spot over Portugal after draw in battle of World Cup heavyweights
excerpt: While wonderful and typical theatre, it was plainly ridiculous. “I’m back!” roared Cristiano Ronaldo, as if playing a key part in Portugal’s dismissal of Uzbekistan was in any way sig
summary: While wonderful and typical theatre, it was plainly ridiculous. “I’m back!” roared Cristiano Ronaldo, as if playing a key part in Portugal’s dismissal of Uzbekistan was in any way significant. Ronaldo toiled against DR Congo and was comfortably kept quiet here by Colombia. The stars have turned up at this World Cup. Ronaldo, though, still has a way to go if seeking to prove his omnipresence at this tournament will not hinder Portuguese hopes of glory. Nobody other than Father Time has chased down Ronaldo. An alternative quote, from Trainspotting, feels more apposite than what the 41-year-old bawled into a camera as Uzbekistan were swatted aside. “Well, at one time, you’ve got it, then you lose it, and it’s gone for ever,” observed Sick Boy in an Edinburgh park. Add Ronaldo to a list that, as per the film scene, includes David Bowie, Lou Reed, Charlie Nicholas and Malcolm McLaren. Eclectic company. Ronaldo actually wants to be in the same discussion as Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland. He once was. Now? It is a tricky point to enforce. Colombia were the better team in this entertaining fixture. It was curious, in fact, that it ended scoreless. Colombia believed they had won the match in stoppage time, with a Davinson Sánchez header that was ruled out for offside. If at a more material World Cup moment, it would be a decision to create rather a lot of fuss. Colombia must improve on ruthlessness if seeking to make a proper World Cup impact. Still, topping this sect...

summary: Presenter Gigi Salmon discusses the 21 Brits across the two draws at Wimbledon, including Jack Draper's blockbuster meeting with Taylor Fritz and she picks her winners; watch the ATP and WTA Tours live on Sky Sports Tennis, streaming service NOW and the Sky Sports app Gigi previews Wimbledon with a total of 21 Britons in singles action. The Sky Sports presenter also looks forward to the return of 23-time major winner Serena Williams at the All England Club and picks her winners! It's all in her latest column... I'm writing this around 3,500 miles away from the All England Club and the Wimbledon Championships, having just spent the afternoon watching a former Wimbledon champion play on a grass-court in New York's Central Park.

title: Austria survive huge scare to progress with Algeria and knock Iran out
excerpt: Super-sub Sasa Kalajdzic saved Austria's World Cup with a last-gasp equaliser after Algeria had stunned their Group J rivals deep into stoppage time in a thrilling 3-3 draw, which saw both sides progress to the last 32 and Iran knocked out.
summary: GEHA Field at Arrowhead StadiumAttendance: Attendance69,045. Match report as Austria come up with last-gasp equaliser after being floored by Riyad Mahrez's injury-time strike which would have knocked them out of World Cup; Draw sees both progress to last 32 where Group J runners-up Austria face Spain and third-place Algeria meet Switzerland Super-sub Sasa Kalajdzic saved Austria's World Cup with a last-gasp equaliser after Algeria had stunned their Group J rivals deep into stoppage time in a thrilling 3-3 draw, which saw both sides progress to the last 32 and Iran knocked out.

title: Predict the World Cup knockout stages! Plot who will lift the trophy...
excerpt: England are through to the knockout rounds of the World Cup - and you can predict how the rest of the tournament will play out!
summary: England are through to the knockout rounds of the World Cup - and you can predict how the rest of the tournament will play out! Thomas Tuchel's side face a last-32 tie against DR Congo on Wednesday before potential fixtures with Mexico, Brazil, Argentina and a final against Spain based on FIFA world rankings. But will it all go according to plan, or do you think there will be some surprises as the pressure ramps up in the closing stages of the tournament?
